CROSS-EXAMINATION BY COUNSEL FOR THE RESPONDENT
QUESTION OF SENATOR-JUDGE P ANGELIN AN

blocks.text: Republic of the Philippines
SENATE
Pasay City
JOURNAL
Senate Sitting as An IMPEACHMENT COURT On the Impeachment Trial oe Vice-President Sara Zimmerman Duterte
IMPEACHMENT TRIAL NO. 6
Tuesday, 14 July 2026
PREPARED BY THE Legislative journal Service Senate of the Philippines
IMPEACHMENT TRIAL NO. 6
Tuesday, July 14, 2026
CALL TO ORDER

At 2:00 p.m., Tuesday, July 14, 2026, the Senate President, Hon. Win Gatchalian, with the Senate sitting as an Impeachment Court, called the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte to order.
PROCLAMATION OF THE SERGEANT-AT-ARMS

The Sergeant-at-Arms, PMGen Alfred S. Corpus, commanded all persons, on pain of penalty, to keep silent while the Senate is sitting for the trial of the Articles of Impeachment against Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte.
PRAYER

blocks.speaker: Sen. Christopher Lawrence T
blocks.text: Go led the prayer, to wit:

Almighty and loving God, O Allah, we come before You today with humility, mindful of the solemn duty entrusted to us and the weight of the decisions that lie ahead. Grant us wisdom to discern what is right, courage to uphold the truth, and fairness in every judgment we make. Keep us from prejudice, anger, personal interest, and political pressure. Help us listen with patience, speak with honesty, and act according to conscience, the Constitution, and the welfare of the Filipino people.

Gabayan Ninyo kaming lahat ng may tungkulin sa mga pagdinig na ito. Nawa ay ang bawat panig ay mabigyan ng pagkakataong marinig, ang bawat ebidensya ay mapag-aralang mabuti, at ang bawat pasya ay nakabatay sa katotohanan, katarungan, at tamang proseso.

Remind us that public office is a sacred trust. May we never lose sight of the people whose lives are affected by our words, our actions, and our decisions. Let the pursuit of truth strengthen our institutions and deepen our commitment to serve with integrity and compassion.

Lord, bring healing to our nation. Where there is division, grant us understanding, patience, and unity. Where there is distrust, restore faith. Where there is fear and anger, give us calmness and restraint.

When these proceedings are concluded, help us move forward together. Nawa ay makabalik na kami sa aming pangunahing tungkulin: ang magserbisyo at maglingkod sa ating mga kababayan. Nawa ay maituon naming muli ang aming buong pansin sa paglilingkod sa ating mga kababayan, lalo na sa mga higit na nangangailangan.
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026

Lastly, may the Philippine Senate remain united in fulfilling its role as the last bastion of democracy. Within these Halls, may truth prevail, and the true voice of the Filipinos be heard. May compassion for others be a steadfast virtue we live by in our words and in our actions, and may we. Your senator-judges, be instruments of Your Grace in the pursuit of peace, justice, and the common good.
All these we ask in Your Holy Name.
Amen.
ROLL CALL
Upon direction of the Senate President, the Clerk of Court, Atty. Renato N. Bantug Jr., called the roll, to which the following senator-judges responded: Padilla, R. Pangilinan, F. N.
Sotto in, V. c. Tulfo, E. T. Tulfo, R. T. Villanueva, J. Villar, C. A. Villar, M. A. Zubiri, J. M. F.
Aquino, B. Ejercito, J. V. Escudero, F. G. Gatchalian, W. T. Go, C. L. T. Hontiveros, R. Lacson, P. M. Lapid, M. M. Legarda, L. Marcos, I. R.
With 19 Senator-Judges present, the Chair declared the presence of a quorum.
Senator-Judges Cayetano (A) and Cayetano (P) were also present.
Senator-Judges Estrada and Marcoleta were under detention and unable to attend.
Senator-Judge Dela Rosa was absent.
APPROVAL OF THE JOURNAL

Upon motion of the Presiding Officer, Senator Francis “Chiz” G. Escudero, there being no objection, the Body dispensed with the reading of the Journal of the Senate sitting as an Impeachment Court on July 13, 2026 and considered the same as approved.
CALLING OF THE CASE

Upon direction of the Presiding Officer, the Clerk of Court, Atty. Renato N. Bantug Jr., called Case No. 004-2026, titled “In the Matter of the Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Zimmerman Duterte. ”
APPEARANCES
The Presiding Officer directed the parties to enter their appearances.

blocks.speaker: Representative Lorenz R
blocks.text: Defensor entered the appearances previously entered, on behalf of the Prosecution.
Atty. Mark Vinluan entered the same appearances on behalf of the Respondent.
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026
PRESENTATION OF WITNESS

blocks.speaker: Presiding Officer Escudero recognized Atty
blocks.text: Jeremy C. Lotoc, Regional Director of the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), to continue his testimony. He also reminded Attorney Lotoc that he was testifying/under the same oath from the previous day’s proceedings.
Upon request of Representative Defensor, Atty. Amando Virgil D. Ligutan, was recognized as Counsel for the Prosecution.
Atty. Mark Vinluan appeared as Counsel for the Respondent.
CROSS-EXAMINATION BY THE COUNSEL FOR THE RESPONDENT {Continuation)
Attorney Vinluan continued his cross-examination of the witness:

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Good afternoon, Attorney Lotoc.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Good afternoon. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: So, yesterday you identified videos, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: And specifically, portions of the November 23, 2024 presscon.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. Now, in that presscon, VP Sara spoke about a lot of topics.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Uh-huh, right.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: In other words—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Mr. Witness, kindly answer yes or no, and not simply say “Uh-huh.”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir. Sorry, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: In other words, she said many statements in that presscon.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: She said many statements, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. My question is this: do you know for sure that VP Sara’s statements during that presscon happened in real life?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: In real life? Well—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Objection, Your Honor, incompetent. The statements happening in real life as to the real life of the Vice President, this witness is being asked that question, of course, the witness is incompetent. Your Honor, please.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel, Counsel, kindly reform. It seems the witness did not understand the question though.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Let me—let me help you. I am showing you also a portion of our Exhibit “D- 1-145-A,” this is the whole version of the video that you—the portions that you identified yesterday. And particularly, I will show you timestamp 51:07 onwards of the video. In this video, the Vice President mentioned about events that happened inside the Batasang Pambansa. Can you play that video, please?
[Video presentation]

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. My question to you is this—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, point of clarification. If I may. Your Honor, please.
/V
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for the Prosecution is recognized.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, there is a video that is marked in evidence, capturing the events on November 23, 2024, and here we have the Defense—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, may we just know the objection. Your Honor?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Wait, wait, I am still explaining.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: This is not a time for lecture. Your Honor.
The Presiding Officer, Counsel, I have not yet recognized you. Let Counsel for the Prosecution finish.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, point of clarification. They are presenting a video with anecdotes. I am now questioning the propriety of the Defense presenting a video without laying the basis where this video came from, who is the source of those annotations thereto. Because, Your Honor, there is a video, they are confi-onting this witness with that video by presenting their own annotated—it is like a prop. Your Honor, please. This is highly irregular in the court of law.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for the Respondent, is this the video previously marked in evidence?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, as I mentioned, we marked it in evidence as “D-4-3,” Your Honor. And this was also marked by the Prosecution as their own. They have a separate marking for this. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: No, no.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: This is part of the two-hour and so-and-so minute video.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Correct, Your Honor. At any rate. Your Honor—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: If I may be allowed to finish.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for the Prosecution, kindly hold on.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: At any rate. Your Honor—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for the Respondent, the Chair is not yet done, and the aimotation below the video, is that part of the marked video?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: This is part. Your Honor, of a separate exhibit that is also part of the Articles of Impeachment, Annex G, Your Honor. This is Mr. Calilung’s own transcription of the video—just to be clarified on that matter.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: But who put it there?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: We just placed it there. Your Honor. So, we will—we are making him identify, actually, the video as well as the appropriate transcription, which are both part of the Articles of Impeachment and Annex G-9.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: The Presiding Officer will allow it. The wimess may answer.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: So, the question is: Can you confirm, for certain, that these statements—or any part thereof— happened in real life?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: And can you give me the timestamp. Your Honor, para ma-compare ko siya kung—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Timestamp 51:07.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Your Honor, if I may refer to my notes—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: You may do so. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Anong timestamp, Sir?
TUESDAY, JULY 14,2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: To help this honorable Court, Your Honor, it appears on page 84, or more or less, of the Articles of Impeachment, Annex G-9, Your Honor. This is the transcription of Agent John Mark Calilung dated December 26, 2024, for the Record.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Noted.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, in the interest of time, can we confront the witness with our own copy?

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Your own copy of what. Counsel?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Of the Articles of Impeachment, Aimex G-9, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Well, Assisting Counsel for the Prosecution. If there... if in case there are any problems, kindly confirm if indeed that is the document being shown to the witness.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: That is page 14 of the Affidavit of Transcription.

Your Honor, I manifest that we are showing that specific portion on the transcript prepared by Agent Calilung of the NBI. But actually. Your Honor, even without consultation with the transcript, my question is very simple. My only question is: Does he know for certain that these statements, or any part of these statements, happened in real life?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Your Honor, nabasa ko po rito sa copy ko, ‘yung nandiyan.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: So, do you confirm that the statements were made by the Vice President, Mr. Witness?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: It’s here in the transcription. Your Honor, yes.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: No, no. My question is not whether she made the statement. My question is: If you know, based on your personal knowledge, if any statement or a portion thereof happened in real life, that’s the question.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, Your Honor, the fact that we have the video on November 26 when the Vice President reiterated what she uttered in November 23, 2024—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, I’m not even—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Let’s allow the witness to answer.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Your Honor, we invited the media personalities who have personal knowledge on that date and on that online press conference, and noong tinanong namin sila kung nag- participate sila, sabi nila yes, nakausap nila yung Rise Presidente, nagbigay sila ng mga katanungan. So, definitely, yes, nangyari po iyon.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, may we manifest—Your Honor, make a manifestation.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Yes, Counsel—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, it appears that the witness is not being responsive to our question. He’s talking about something that I did not ask. We are only focusing on this particular statement. We will show other statements later on, and we cannot get an answer from him—if he knows.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel, even I am confused with the question “happened in real life.” I think some of us are too.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: I’ll be more specific. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: The question is, “kung alam n’yo bang nangyari ito sa totoong buhay? " Ang sagot ng testigo ay sa pagkakaalam niya ay “Oo, ” base sa kanilang imbestigasyon at sinabi ng mga inimbitahan nilang testigo.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. I’ll clarify the question. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Kindly do so, Counsel—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. In the statement, for example, the Vice President said, “There were threats to my life inside the Batasang Pambansa.” Are you confirming that?
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: That is within the transcription here, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. Now, my question to you is this: Did you investigate those threats?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Objection—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: I’m not yet done.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: All right.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: I’m not yet done.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Did you investigate the threat to VP Sara’s life during that time?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Objection. Now, misleading. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: What is—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Because the question was: “Do you confirm that that statement is found in the transcription as to the Vice President saying that there were threats to her life?’’ And then the witness said, “Yes, I confirm that,”—”I can find it in the transcription.” Now, the next question of the good Counsel is: “So, did you investigate the threat?” That is the classic misleading question.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Can we get a ruling. Your Honor?

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel, he’s a lawyer. Attorney Lotoc is a lawyer and he knows exactly what’s going on.
Attorney Lotoc, kindly answer.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: What’s—what’s your answer?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: What’s the question. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: The question is: Did you in— she mentioned there about the threats to her life. My question is simple: Did you investigate the threats to VP Sara’s life during that time?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: You mean during the livestream. Sir?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: During that time, correct. During that livestream. She mentioned that there were threats to her life.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, based on our investigation, nangyari yung livestream between 11:00 PM ng 22 to 1:00 AM ng 23, and then nalaman namin noong kumalat siya sa social media, mga hapon na, kaya nag-start kami ng investigation. At that time, hindi pa namin alam 'yung buong laman ng— ng video na iyan, kasi nag-start pa lang kami. So, I instructed Agent Calilung na siya ang maging agent-on-case, and then kunin niya ‘yung video, the whole video, para sa authentication.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Attorney Lotoc, kindly answer the question directly.
So, I get from your long answer that the answer is “no.”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Specifically, at that livestream moment na ho, Sir, hindi pa namin alam 'yung—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: That was the question of Attorney Vinluan— if you investigated at that time. So, the answer is “no.”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Okay. Please proceed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: But later on, you said you did an investigation. So, are you admitting that there were threats to the life of VP Sara?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Precisely. That’s the reason why we sent the December 2, 2024 letter for VP Sara to shed light on our investigation.
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. I’ll move on to a different part of the video.

On page— also of Exhibit D-1-145-A, this now pertains to timestamp 59:30 onwards of the video recording. So, page 15 of the Affidavit of Transcription, also by Agent Calilung of the NBI, dated December 26, 2024. Can we show that video and then provide the appropriate transcript? Can you play that again, please?
[ Video presentation]

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. So, my question to you is this. Having watched that video and read the transcript, my question is very simple, “Do you know for sure if this statement by the Vice President, or any part thereof, happened in real life?”

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Well, Your Honor, as I’ve said to the Counsel already, noong inimbestigahan namin yan, ‘yung video—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, a “yes” or “no” answer will suffice. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: ...Kindly answer more directly to save the Court’s time. Attorney Lotoc.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Your Honor, based on our investigation, yes, it happens in the natural order of things. Nangyari talaga iyan.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: So, in other words, you’re admitting that Martin Romualdez goes around the country to distribute money?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: I’m saying, nangyari ho iyong utterances na iyan.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: That’s not my question. That’s precisely my point. I’m not asking if she made these statements. I’m asking if the truth of these statements you have personal knowledge of So, be more direct in your answer.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: That’s why I have to object now. Your Honor.

Now, there are several statements made and then the witness is being asked, do you confirm the statements? Compound question. Exactly, that’s the reason why we don’t allow. There are so many statements—o sinasabi, gumawa ng batas. So, siguro iyong tanong is, is this... this... this happened—that did this happen in real life. So, this witness being confronted with several statements and he is being asked to answer a yes or no. That’s why it’s compound question.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, my question is the statements or any part thereof. Your Honor. The witness is smart enough to answer. He can just clarify.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: The objection is sustained but not on that ground but rather on the ground that the wimess is incompetent to testify as to the truth of the matter stated by the Vice President. He can only testify as to the fact that those statements were made only as to tenor but not as to the truth. Given that he only investigated and was not there.
You may proceed. Counsel.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: In that case. Your Honor, if we confront also—just a manifestation. Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Yes. You may proceed.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: In other words. Your Honor—we would like to manifest. Your Honor, based on that ruling of the Court that even if VP Sara said that she already talked to a person, then we cannot confirm the truth because this witness does not have personal knowledge.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: That’s a conclusion. Objection.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: That’s just a manifestation for the Court’s appreciation. Can I just proceed with my next question. Your Honor?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Counter manifestation. Your Honor, please. To put into record. Your Honor, please.
The Presiding Officer . For about 15 seconds. Counsel, yes.
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. The defense is entitled to their own conception of things. They’re entitled to their own imagination. That’s all.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel may proceed with his cross.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Just to belabor this point, Your Honor. Aimex D-I-145-A—anyway, Your Honor, they showed around seven to eight videos yesterday. So, we’ll show about four. This pertains to timestamp 1:08:58 and we will also present page 17 of the affidavit of transcription also by Agent Calilung dated December 26, 2024.
In this video clip, the Vice President mentioned about the former speaker of the House and the confidential funds of the Office of the President.
Can we play that video?
[Video presentation]

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. Again, my question based on this video is very simple. She spoke about Speaker Martin Romualdez as well as the President.
Can you confirm or do you know for sure that these statements about Martin Romualdez or the President are true?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Okay. Your Honor, I will object on the same ground and I hope that the Presiding Officer will do the same ruling. This wimess is incompetent. I know what the defense is doing. Your Honor. Objection, incompetent. This witness is highly incompetent as to whether he knows, whether those statements are, in fact, happening.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: What’s the ground for the objection? Can we just be informed of the ground for the objection?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: I just said it, incompetent. This witness— is incompetent.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Can we get the ruling. Your Honor?

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: The Chair made an earlier ruling already that the witness is incompetent to testify as to the truth of the matter but since Attorney Lotoc, you’re a lawyer, I will give it this time to the Counsel for the Respondent. Kindly respond since you’re a lawyer. You should know the proper answer to this question. Kindly respond to the question of the Counsel for respondent.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Again, I do not know personally about the factual circumstances. What I know is that she uttered those statements.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: We are satisfied with that answer. Your Honor. One last video.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Proceed, Counsel.

blocks.speaker: Mr, Vinluan
blocks.text: One last video. On page 87 of the Articles of Impeachment, Annex G9. This is timestamp 1:12:43. This is quite a lengthy video but not as lengthy but this is our last video so we beg the Court’s indulgence. The affidavit of transcription, page 17, also of Agent Calilung, dated December 26,2024.
Are we ready with that video?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Your Honor, to save—

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: Counsel for the prosecutors.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: —because I know Counsel will ask the same question. So, to save time. Your Honor, please since Counsel—

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, what is the objection?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Wait. I’m not yet done. I’m not yet done.
The Presiding Officer^. Kindly let the Counsel for the prosecutor finish.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: Relax. Now, Your Honor, if the same line of question will be asked, okay—
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Your Honor, he cannot preempt, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: I’m not yet done. I’m not yet done.
The prosecution is willing to stipulate that, yes, the Vice President, uttered that statement as to the fact of her uttering the statement.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: We have a different question, Your Honor, just to satisfy his query.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Ligutan
blocks.text: All right.

blocks.speaker: The Presiding Officer
blocks.text: So, I guess the objection is withdrawn and the manifestation is withdrawn. Counsel for respondent may proceed for the showing of—the playing of the video.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Play the video, please.
[Video presentation]

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: Attorney Lotoc, she said a lot of statements in that portion of the video.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor, correct.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: In fact, that statement included the statement which you investigated, correct?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Included in the transcription, Sir, yes.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. Now, you get the point. My question to you is this; Can you confirm now that the statements she made about BBM, FL, and the former Speaker and the Congressmen relating to various acts of corruption are true and happened in real life?

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: As to the existence of those utterances, yes, Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan
blocks.text: All right. So, you just admitted that the Congressmen are all corrupt.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: As to the existence of utterances, yes. Sir.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Vinluan

MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A)
blocks.text: This is a manifestation and point of information.

As Senator-Judge Lacson said, it’s for each judge to appreciate. But let me put context. I’m not close to VP Sara. I’m not close to Mayor Baste. Ang kasama ko sa Congress si former Deputy Speaker Pulong. But in the last four years, I think I’ve seen him twice or thrice.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Lotoc
blocks.text: Yes, Your Honor.

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A)
blocks.text: Okay. But my reading noong sinabi ni Mayor Baste is, “Pag natulog ka sa gabi, remember the Romanovs.” So, we have to look at our history. The Romanovs were evicted via a revolution, di ba? So, Mayor Baste was doing a parallel. Noong 1986, napaalis iyong mga Marcoses via a revolution. But unlike the Romanovs, the Marcoses were given asylum, or whatever you call it, in Hawaii, and over time nakabalik until the President became President. Ang Romanovs, inubos sila para nga hindi makabalik.

Now, if Mayor Baste was making Romanovs uubusin kay Marcos, dapat na-offend si Senator- Judge Imee R. Marcos, because she’s a Marcos. So secondly, if he made that threat—so if he was saying, “May Oplan Romanov, or wara-Romanov kayo," I’m sure the PNP or the Presidential Security Command, the National Council, would have filed a case against Baste. So, I just want— it’s fair that you mentioned him, it’s fair sa investigation mo, but I don’t think we should draw a conclusion here that Mayor Baste was threatening the First Family because remember, there’s a thin line between a warning and a threat. So, let’s just be circumspect and careful from making conclusions. But as far as it is relevant, Mr. Presiding Officer, I appreciate the answer of the witness.

So, the prosecution must always prove iyong elements of the crime.

Ang question ko ay: in your appreciation, dapat ba na ang supposed victim nakaramdam talaga siya ng threats, or is it enough that the general public considers the statement threatening? Okay, so is it personal, or is it a general sentiment?

So, actus reus. Your Honor, means whether the offender in fact uttered the threatening remarks. We were able to establish that, that’s why ^-authenticate po ng NBI iyong video na hung saan nandodoon yung utterances. The test of mens rea. Your Honor, what we did: we interpret the utterances objectively, and in the light of the circumstances in which they were uttered, in the manner in which those utterances were spoken, the person to whom they were addressed, the utterances were addressed, the relationship between the parties—meaning the offender and the recipients—and then the reactions of the recipients, whether they treat the utterances serious or real.

But again, I would agree with what Attorney Ligutan said, in fact, ang basis po namin diyan, yung Garma versus People. Ang isa sa sinabi ng Garma versus People, hindi ho kailangan na ma-intimidate o itrato ng recipient na seryoso yung threat na yan. An admission against interest under the rules is simply an exception to the hearsay rule, where a person is about to die or is unavailable to testify, his statements against his or her interest may be admitted, testified to by another person, not the way we seem to be giving admission against interest na parang umamin na, dahil labag sa interes niya iyon. The rules are specific, and the purpose also is specific. It is an exception to the hearsay rule. Noting that the memoranda comprised approximately 60 pages, Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) inquired whether the Court would rule immediately after the oral arguments or deliberate further before rendering its decision on Monday. Presiding Officer Escudero replied that the Court intended to decide the matter the following day to give the parties ample time to examine records produced in case the subpoenas were granted, adding that the requested documents were in relation to Article II, the hearing of which would only begin in August.While Presiding Officer Escudero confirmed that under the current schedule, it was the last article to be heard, the Prosecution has the discretion to alter the sequence of its presentation.

Clarifying that he was not proposing to defer the oral arguments, Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) suggested that the senator-judges first hold a caucus and deliberate before voting on the requests. He noted that a similar procedure had been followed in a previous impeachment trial and that the issues involving the AMLC and BIR records could establish important precedents. Senate President Gatchalian agreed with Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) that the parties’ memoranda were lengthy and complicated, requiring review by the senator-judges and their legal teams. He initially proposed holding a caucus at 10:00 a.m. the following day.

MANIFESTATION OF ATTORNEY KAPUNAN

Attorney Loma P. Kapunan, Counsel for the Prosecution, manifested that the Prosecution would no longer present Attorney Zuleika Lopez and Capt. Belinda Bello as witnesses. She explained that the Prosecution considered their testimonies unnecessary in light of admissions made by the Defense, after four trial days of testimony, the presentation of 19 documentary exhibits authenticated by NBI Agent John Mark Calilung and 45 documentary exhibits authenticated by Atty. Lotoc, together with the questions of the senator-judges.

She further explained that Attorney Lopez’s proposed testimony had become unnecessary because the Defense had effectively admitted the Vice President’s statements, the presence of the media, and the authenticity of the video, including by relying on the same video as evidence. She emphasized that the Impeachment Court’s role is not to determine criminal liability for Grave Threats or Inciting to Sedition, but to decide whether the Respondent’s admitted acts constitute impeachable offenses under the Constitution. She further stated that Captain Bello’s testimony would have been merely corroborative, limited to the detention transfer order and its relation to the Vice President’s November 23, 2024 press conference.
She added that dispensing with the two witnesses would promote the Respondent’s right to a speedy trial and expressed hope that the defense would adopt the same approach
REMARKS OF SENATOR-JUDGE GATCHALIAN

Senate President Gatchalian expressed gratitude to the Prosecution for dispensing with the presentation of two witnesses, noting that it would help expedite the proceedings. He observed that the pre-trial order listed 57 prosecution witnesses and 45 defense witnesses, or 102 witnesses in total, and estimated that, at the current pace of two hearing days per witness, the trial could last about 17 months. He thus encouraged both parties to continue adopting measures that would shorten the proceedings.
ORDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Presiding Officer Escudero thanked the Prosecution for dispensing with the two witnesses and announced that the July 15, 2026 hearing would instead be devoted to the oral arguments on the Prosecution’s motions for subpoenas covering bank, AMLC, and BIR records. With NBI Director Melvin Matibag’s testimony reset to July 20, 2026, he directed that the motions be argued jointly in accordance with the parties’ consolidated memoranda. He initially allotted 10 minutes per side for the principal arguments and three minutes each for rebuttal and surrebuttal, and informed the Body that the senator-judges would deliberate in caucus before the Court ruled on the motions.
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026
MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE CAYETANO (A)

Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) proposed extending the time for oral arguments to 15 to 20 minutes per side, emphasizing the importance of the subpoena motions and the need to distinguish legitimate requests for evidence from a fishing expedition. He added that more comprehensive presentations would better assist both the Court and the public and could reduce the need for questions.
ORDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

Upon the parties’ manifestation, Presiding Officer Escudero approved 15 minutes per side for the principal arguments and 10 minutes each for rebuttal and surrebuttal. He likewise reminded the senator-judges that, under the Rules, each may speak for up to 10 minutes, subject to an aggregate limit of one hour per side during plenary debate, if necessary, and urged the parties to maximize their allotted time.
INQUIRY OF SENATOR-JUDGE SOTTO

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Sotto sought clarification on the application of the time limits for the senator- judges
blocks.text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) explained that the extended speaking time would apply only

if the Court failed to reach a consensus in caucus. Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the two-minute limit applied only to clarificatory questions during evidentiary proceedings, clarifying that in this case, both sides will be given enough time to present their arguments in chief and on rebuttal. The Senators will have the opportunity to listen and thereafter go into caucus. Failing a consensus in caucus, a debate may ensue for a maximum of two hours - one hour for each side. He also ruled that the AMLC and BIR subpoena motions would be heard jointly because the parties had submitted consolidated memoranda. Senator-Judge Sotto then suggested deferring any adjustment of the senator-judges’ speaking time until after the caucus, to which the Presiding Officer agreed.
MANIFESTATION OF SENATOR-JUDGE PANGILINAN

blocks.speaker: Senator-Judge Pangilinan clarified for the record that People v
blocks.text: Mas was not dismissed on the merits. He explained that the Regional Trial Court (RTC) dismissed the case on June 24 after granting a Motion to Quash based on unlawful arrest, resulting in the court’s lack of jurisdiction over the accused, and emphasized that the dismissal was purely procedural and did not resolve the Inciting to Sedition charge on the merits.
ORDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER
Presiding Officer Escudero directed Representative Defensor to submit the Prosecution’s witness list for the following week’s hearings the next day.

In response to the Presiding Officer’s inquiry. Representative Defensor confirmed that no subpoena would be necessary for the appearance of NBI Director Melvin Matibag on Monday, July 20, 2026. Thereafter, Attorney Vinluan manifested that the Respondent had no further matters to raise.

At the Prosecution’s request, the Presiding Officer directed the Assisting Counsel for both parties to reseal the USB devices opened during the witness’s testimony after adjournment. Representative Defensor then manifested that the Prosecution had no further matters for the Court’s consideration.
TUESDAY, JULY 14, 2026
ADJOURNMENT OF THE IMPEACHMENT TRIAL
The Presiding Officer declared the impeachment trial adjourned until two o’clock in the afternoon of Wednesday, July 15, 2026.
It was 7:41 p.m.
I hereby certify to the correctness of the foregoing.
TUG JR.
NATO NyBAp Clerk of/Coujt Approved on July 15, 2026
